title: mentha aquatica var. denticulata
intro: Mentha aquatica var. denticulata, also known as Japanese mint, is a variety of water mint that belongs to the Lamiaceae family. It has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include its ability to act as an antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant. The health benefits of Japanese mint include relief from digestive issues, headaches, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve oral health. Japanese mint can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote well-being and alleviate various health concerns.
